headline: Verizon Communications (VZ) Stock Sinks As Market Gains: Here's Why
summary: Verizon Communications (VZ) concluded the recent trading session at $50.37, signifying a -1.06% move from its prior day's close.

headline: How The Verizon (VZ) Investment Story Is Shifting On Costs Transparency And Fair Value Estimates
summary: Verizon Communications now carries an updated analyst fair value estimate of US$50.76, compared with the prior US$49.99, a modest shift that has caught investor attention. This change sits against a backdrop of mixed research commentary, where some firms are lifting price targets on the back of subscriber trends and cost actions, while others remain cautious due to concerns around disclosure quality and reporting changes. Read on to see how you can track these moving pieces and keep up with...

headline: How Investors May Respond To Verizon (VZ) Settling Fractus Patent Dispute And Easing Legal Overhang
summary: Verizon Communications recently resolved a patent infringement lawsuit with Fractus related to antenna technology used in Internet of Things applications, removing a legal dispute that had been ongoing since December 2024. This settlement, combined with Verizon’s emphasis on expanding fixed wireless access and maintaining its long record of dividend increases, may influence how investors assess the company’s risk profile and cash-flow resilience. With the Fractus patent settlement now behind...

headline: Verizon Receives a Vote of Confidence as Goldman Sachs Raises Price Target
summary: Verizon Communications (NYSE:VZ) has been on a meaningful run, with shares up 2.78% over the past month and up nearly 25% year-to-date. The stock is pushing towards its 52-week high of $51.68, which it set earlier in March. Shares are currently trading around $50.60. headline: Fractus and Verizon settle patent litigation
summary: Fractus, the pioneer in antenna technology and IP licensing, today announced that it has reached a settlement with Verizon, resolving the patent infringement litigation Fractus filed in December 2024 in the United States District Court for the Eastern District of Texas (Case No. 2:24-cv-01009).
